title: Symplocos polyandra
taxonomy: Symplocos polyandra is a member of the family Symplocaceae. This family is a part of the order Ericales. Symplocaceae is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the sweetleaf or bead tree family. The order Ericales is a large and diverse group of flowering plants that also includes the families of blueberry, azalea, and heath. Symplocos polyandra is a species of flowering plant native to the Himalayan region and parts of Asia.
uses: Symplocos polyandra is used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The leaves and bark of Symplocos polyandra are used to make medicinal preparations, such as decoctions and infusions. However, more research is needed to fully understand the medicinal properties and potential uses of Symplocos polyandra.
